input.query: ESP32 Arduino encoder ISR digitalRead vs direct GPIO register quadrature accuracy 2024
ESP32 quadrature encoder IRAM_ATTR digitalRead latency interrupt accuracy

content.content:
Web search results for query: "ESP32 Arduino encoder ISR digitalRead vs direct GPIO register quadrature accuracy 2024"

Links: [{"title":"ESP32 GPIO Interrupts - need some help.","url":"https://forum.dronebotworkshop.com/esp32-esp8266/esp32-gpio-interrupts-need-some-help/"},{"title":"ESP32 - Rotary Encoder | ESP32 Tutorial","url":"https://esp32io.com/tutorials/esp32-rotary-encoder"},{"title":"esp32-rotary-encoder: Main Page","url":"https://davidantliff.github.io/esp32-rotary-encoder/index.html"},{"title":"GitHub - DavidAntliff/esp32-rotary-encoder: ESP32-compatible rotary encoder driver","url":"https://github.com/DavidAntliff/esp32-rotary-encoder"},{"title":"ESP32 KY-040 Rotary Encoder Module Pinout, Wiring, ESP32 and more","url":"https://www.espboards.dev/sensors/ky-040/"},{"title":"Arduino","url":"https://docs.arduino.cc/libraries/esp32encoder/"},{"title":"Using Interrupts and Global Flags to Read a Rotary Encoder on ESP32: A Guide","url":"https://copyprogramming.com/howto/how-to-read-a-rotary-encoder-with-interrupts-on-esp32-and-global-flags"},{"title":"GitHub - madhephaestus/ESP32Encoder: A Quadrature and half quadrature PCNT peripheral driven encoder library supporting 8 encoders","url":"https://github.com/madhephaestus/ESP32Encoder"},{"title":"Quadrature Decoding with Arduino – New Screwdriver","url":"https://newscrewdriver.com/2024/02/01/quadrature-decoding-with-arduino/"},{"title":"How to connect Optical Encoder with ESP32 - Electric DIY Lab","url":"https://electricdiylab.com/how-to-connect-optical-encoder-with-esp32/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding ESP32 encoder ISR implementation with digitalRead versus direct GPIO register access for quadrature accuracy:

## Key Findings:

**Interrupt-Based Approaches:**
Using a state machine to decode the sequence of quadrature states provides a fairly glitch-free and accurate measurement of the encoder's movement.

**digitalRead in ISR Context:**
According to some developers, digitalRead should become unnecessary when utilizing interrupts. However, this approach has limitations. Using flag-based approaches without proper state reading can lead to incorrect direction detection, particularly when the shaft completes a single step while performing two opposite actions.

**Direct Register Access Approach:**
Alternative implementations use direct GPIO register functions like `gpio_get_level()` instead of `digitalRead()` within ISR handlers for more efficient encoder reading.

**Hardware Acceleration Option:**
For higher accuracy and better performance, the ESP32's pulse counter (PCNT) peripheral is available as an alternative to software ISR-based approaches. The PCNT peripheral can support full and half quadrature modes, and single edge count mode.

**Performance Considerations:**
ISR overhead on microcontrollers can be significant, with work done before any ISR code actually runs putting a hard upper bound on response rates.

The search results indicate that direct GPIO register access (like `gpio_get_level()`) is generally preferred over `digitalRead()` in ISRs for better performance, but using the hardware PCNT peripheral is often the most accurate solution for quadrature encoding on ESP32.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "ESP32 quadrature encoder IRAM_ATTR digitalRead latency interrupt accuracy"

Links: [{"title":"ESP32 GPIO Interrupts - need some help.","url":"https://forum.dronebotworkshop.com/esp32-esp8266/esp32-gpio-interrupts-need-some-help/"},{"title":"using pulse counter to read optical (quadrature) decoder? - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://www.esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=4983"},{"title":"ESP32 encoder quadrature/rotary encoder - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=18569"},{"title":"ESP32 - Rotary Encoder | ESP32 Tutorial","url":"https://esp32io.com/tutorials/esp32-rotary-encoder"},{"title":"Not getting accurate high speed timer interrupt intervals · Issue #3635 · espressif/arduino-esp32","url":"https://github.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/issues/3635"},{"title":"GitHub - madhephaestus/ESP32Encoder: A Quadrature and half quadrature PCNT peripheral driven encoder library supporting 8 encoders","url":"https://github.com/madhephaestus/ESP32Encoder"},{"title":"ESP32 external interrupt latency - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=35329"},{"title":"What is and when to use IRAM_ATTR ? - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=4978"},{"title":"Arduino","url":"https://www.arduino.cc/reference/en/libraries/esp32encoder/"},{"title":"ESP32 KY-040 Rotary Encoder Module Pinout, Wiring, ESP32 and more","url":"https://www.espboards.dev/sensors/ky-040/"}]

I've completed a web search for your query about ESP32 quadrature encoder implementation with IRAM_ATTR, digitalRead, latency, interrupt, and accuracy. Here are the key findings:

## Quadrature Encoder Implementation on ESP32

Quadrature encoders have two channels that generate symmetrical square waves that are 90 degrees out of phase from each other, and counting the RISING and FALLING edges of both channels yields four counts per pulse of the encoder.

## Using IRAM_ATTR for Interrupt Functions

IRAM_ATTR tells a function to use or be in the Internal RAM of the ESP32. The cache error can occur from the flash cache, which is used while accessing flash; if flash is disabled it cannot update itself, hence the error. Using IRAM_ATTR is a valuable solution for reducing latency issues with interrupt functions.

## DigitalRead in ISR

Common implementations use IRAM_ATTR with digitalRead() calls within interrupt service routines to read the state of the encoder pins. It's important to keep the code within the interrupt as straightforward as possible and avoid using Serial.print() or Serial.println() inside the interrupt.

## Hardware Alternative: PCNT Peripheral

The ESP32Encoder library uses the ESP32 pulse counter hardware peripheral with only one interrupt for the peripheral managed by the library, generating interrupts when the 16-bit counter buffer overflows, providing a tiny interrupt footprint while supporting up to 8 simultaneous quadrature encoders.

The search results show practical implementations and best practices for achieving accurate quadrature encoder reading on the ESP32.

input.query: ESP32 digitalRead ISR overhead cycles GPIO_REG_READ performance comparison
Arduino float precision angle calculation 360 degrees rounding error encoder

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Arduino float precision angle calculation 360 degrees rounding error encoder"

Links: [{"title":"Strange Float or Rounding error - Programming - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/strange-float-or-rounding-error/469408"},{"title":"getting angle from encoder - General Electronics - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/getting-angle-from-encoder/286004"},{"title":"How to set encoder to 365 degrees? - General Guidance - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/how-to-set-encoder-to-365-degrees/870081"},{"title":"converting encoder data - Sensors - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/converting-encoder-data/114623"},{"title":"Rotary Encoder in degrees - Sensors - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/rotary-encoder-in-degrees/522466"},{"title":"float precision problems - Programming Questions - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/float-precision-problems/119180"},{"title":"Float Values in Arduino | Forum for Electronics","url":"https://www.edaboard.com/threads/float-values-in-arduino.331020/"},{"title":"Creating an offset angle using an absolute encoder - Programming - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/creating-an-offset-angle-using-an-absolute-encoder/399492"},{"title":"Five Arduino math fixes for when it is wrong - Bald Engineer","url":"https://www.baldengineer.com/arduino-math-fixes.html"},{"title":"How can I increase precision of float display in the Serial Monitor? - Programming - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/how-can-i-increase-precision-of-float-display-in-the-serial-monitor/146107"}]

The search returned results related to Arduino float precision issues and encoder angle calculations. Here's what I found:

## Float Precision Issues in Arduino

On 8-bit AVR Arduino boards, the float type is 32-bits which makes the limit 6 or 7 digits. This is an important consideration when working with angle calculations.

The value 0.10 can only be represented approximately using the IEEE754 single precision floating point standard, and these rounding errors inherent with floating point standards can be an issue.

## Encoder Angle Calculation

Several developers have worked on converting encoder data to degrees. One approach involves converting encoder data with a resolution of 2500 ppr (pulses per revolution) into mechanical degrees, though using factors like (360/2500.0) or (360.0/2500.0*4.0) may not work as expected.

## Recommended Approach

One suggested solution is to use an integer type to count encoder clicks, incrementing and decrementing based on direction, then convert the count to a string with formatted output including a decimal point, as integer types are a good method when counting an event while floating types are typically used to increase the possible range while maintaining precision.

For 360-degree wrapping, many developers use the modulo operator to keep angles within the 0-360 range, though float precision issues should be considered when doing so.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "ESP32 digitalRead ISR overhead cycles GPIO_REG_READ performance comparison"

Links: [{"title":"Fast digital read - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=35933"},{"title":"GPIO & RTC GPIO - ESP32 - — ESP-IDF Programming Guide latest documentation","url":"https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/latest/esp32/api-reference/peripherals/gpio.html"},{"title":"High speed GPIO operations - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=713"},{"title":"ESP32 digitalRead speed - Sensors - Arduino Forum","url":"https://forum.arduino.cc/t/esp32-digitalread-speed/929151"},{"title":"External Interrupt Latency - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://www.esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=422"},{"title":"Really fast timer ISR for GPIO at 1 MHz or faster - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://www.esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=38859"},{"title":"Direct IO Port Manipulation - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://www.esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=1746"},{"title":"ESP32 GPIO Interrupts - need some help.","url":"https://forum.dronebotworkshop.com/esp32-esp8266/esp32-gpio-interrupts-need-some-help/"},{"title":"[Question]: Maximum interrupt rate on GPIO - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=2524"},{"title":"How to use gpio_isr_register? - ESP32 Forum","url":"https://esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=345"}]

I've performed the web search for your query about ESP32 digitalRead ISR overhead, GPIO_REG_READ, and performance comparison. Here are the key findings:

## GPIO Register Access Methods

REG_READ(GPIO_IN_REG) is giving more or less the same performance as the GPIO.in method for fast digital reads on the ESP32.

## ISR Overhead

100-200 CPU cycles of 'overhead' for an ISR call/return might be on the optimistic side. More specifically, the delay from external trigger to application-provided ISR handler is around 2us (at 240MHz clock), which is around 500 cycles.

For minimal interrupt handling, a trivial interrupt handler typically takes ~2 microseconds to execute.

## Performance Optimization Techniques

For faster GPIO reads, you can use register macros like REG_READ(GPIO_IN_REG) >> (PIN_TO_READ) for pins 0-31, or REG_READ(GPIO_IN1_REG) >> (PIN_TO_READ-32) for higher numbered pins.

When reading the "in" register, you get the current logic levels for IO pins 0 through 31 in one go, irrespective of which of the pins are inputs or outputs.

## Newer Chip Improvements

Newer ESP32 chips (ESP32-Cx, ESP32-Sx) have 'fast GPIO' that may help improve performance compared to the classic ESP32.

content.text:
충분한 정보를 수집했습니다. 코드 분석을 진행합니다.

---

# ESP32 인코더 코드 각도 측정 정확도 문제 분석

## 문제 1. `countToDegree` 정확도/정밀도 문제

### 1-1. float 연산의 정밀도 손실

IEEE 754 단정밀도 float는 유효숫자 약 7자리입니다.

```
360.0f / 4096 = 0.087890625f  → float로 정확히 표현 가능
```

그러나 `count`가 누적되면:

```
count = 40960 (10회전) 기준:
  (float)40960 / 4096 * 360.0f
  = 10.0 * 360.0f = 3600.0f  → 정확

count = 4194304 (1024회전) 기준:
  float의 정수 표현 한계: 2^23 = 8388608
  → 이 정도 규모에서는 문제없음
```

실제 위험 구간은 `count` 자체보다 **`deg` 값을 JSON 문자열로 출력할 때 소수점 2자리 반올림**입니다.

```
분해능 = 360 / 4096 = 0.087890625°/count
소수점 2자리 반올림 후 오차: 최대 ±0.005°
측정 범위 2~10°에서 오차 비율: 최대 0.25% (2° 기준) → 수용 가능
```

**하지만 진짜 문제는 아래 1-2입니다.**

### 1-2. `%` 연산자 음수 처리 — C# 계산 오류 유발

코드 자체의 보정은 올바릅니다:

```c
int32_t m = ((count % COUNTS_PER_REV) + COUNTS_PER_REV) % COUNTS_PER_REV;
```

이 수식은 음수 count에서도 0~4095 범위를 보장합니다. C측은 문제없음.

**문제는 C# 쪽 계산**:

```csharp
// 예시: CW 회전, ZERO 후 약 5° 지점 통전
switchAngleStart = 0.09°   (count=1 직후 캡처)
switchAngleEnd   = 4.99°

switchAngle = |4.99 - 0.09| = 4.90°  → 정상

// 문제 케이스: ZERO 근처에서 360° 경계 통과
switchAngleStart = 359.91°  (ZERO 직전 캡처, count=-1)
switchAngleEnd   =   0.09°  (ZERO 직후 캡처, count=+1)

switchAngle = |0.09 - 359.91| = 359.82°  ← 실제는 0.18°여야 함
```

**수정 방법 (C#):**

```csharp
float diff = switchAngleEnd - switchAngleStart;
// 360° 경계 wrap 처리
if (diff > 180f)  diff -= 360f;
if (diff < -180f) diff += 360f;
switchAngle = Math.Abs(diff);
```

---

## 문제 2. ISR에서 `digitalRead` 사용 문제 (가장 심각)

### 2-1. `digitalRead` 의 과도한 오버헤드

Arduino ESP32의 `digitalRead()`는 내부적으로:

1. 핀 번호 → GPIO 번호 변환 (`digitalPinToGPIONumber`)
2. GPIO 모드 검증
3. 레지스터 읽기

이 과정이 **약 1~2μs (240MHz 기준 240~480 사이클)** 를 소요합니다. ISR 진입 오버헤드 약 2μs에 핀 2개 읽기 각각 ~1μs가 더해집니다.

**임계 문제: 두 번의 `digitalRead` 사이에 상태가 바뀔 수 있음**

```c
// 현재 코드 — A 읽은 후 B 읽기까지 ~1μs 공백 존재
uint8_t a = digitalRead(ENC_PIN_A);  // t=0
// ← 이 사이에 신호 변화 발생 가능
uint8_t b = digitalRead(ENC_PIN_B);  // t=+1μs

// A, B가 실제로 동시 상태가 아님 → 잘못된 state 조합 → QUAD_TABLE 오류
```

1024 PPR × 4배율 = 4096 CPR. 예를 들어 100 RPM 회전 시:

```
엣지 발생 주기 = 60 / (100 * 4096) = 146μs
digitalRead 2회 간격 ~1μs → 146분의 1 확률로 state 불일치 발생
1000 RPM 에서는 14.6μs 주기 → 더욱 심각
```

### 2-2. 수정 방법 — GPIO 레지스터 원자적 읽기

두 핀을 **한 레지스터 읽기로 동시에** 캡처합니다:

```c
// 핀 번호가 모두 GPIO 0~31 범위일 때 (GPIO 32~39은 GPIO_IN1_REG 사용)
#define ENC_PIN_A  18
#define ENC_PIN_B  19

void IRAM_ATTR enc_ab_isr() {
  // GPIO_IN_REG 레지스터를 한 번에 읽어 두 핀 동시 캡처
  uint32_t gpio_state = REG_READ(GPIO_IN_REG);
  uint8_t a = (gpio_state >> ENC_PIN_A) & 1;
  uint8_t b = (gpio_state >> ENC_PIN_B) & 1;

  uint8_t cur = (b << 1) | a;
  enc_count += QUAD_TABLE[enc_last_state][cur];
  enc_last_state = cur;
}
```

**또는 ESP32 하드웨어 PCNT 사용 (최선책):**

```c
#include <ESP32Encoder.h>  // madhephaestus/ESP32Encoder

ESP32Encoder encoder;

void setup() {
  // PCNT 하드웨어 주변장치 사용 — ISR 오버헤드 없음
  encoder.attachFullQuad(ENC_PIN_A, ENC_PIN_B);
  encoder.clearCount();
}

int32_t getAbsCount() {
  return (int32_t)encoder.getCount() - enc_offset;
}
```

PCNT는 하드웨어가 쿼드러처를 직접 디코딩하므로 CPU 개입 없이 정확합니다. 단, 16비트 카운터이므로 오버플로 인터럽트 처리가 필요합니다(라이브러리가 자동 처리).

---

## 문제 3. ZERO 명령의 Race Condition

### 3-1. 현재 코드 문제

```c
if (cmd == "ZERO") {
    noInterrupts();
    enc_count = 0;      // ISR 차단 중 초기화
    interrupts();
    enc_offset = 0;     // ← ISR 재개 후 enc_offset 설정 전 공백!
}
```

`interrupts()` 재개 직후, `enc_offset = 0` 실행 전에 ISR이 발생하면:

```
enc_count = 1  (ISR에서 +1)
enc_offset = 0 (이제 설정)
→ getAbsCount() = 1 - 0 = 1  → 0이 아님
```

ZERO 명령의 의도 자체도 모호합니다. `enc_count`를 0으로 초기화한 뒤 `enc_offset`도 0으로 설정하는 것은 동일 효과이지만, **원래 설계 의도(`enc_offset`을 현재 count로 설정하는 방식)가 더 안전**합니다.

### 3-2. 수정 방법

```c
if (cmd == "ZERO") {
    noInterrupts();
    enc_offset = enc_count;  // 현재 count를 기준점으로 설정
    // enc_count는 건드리지 않음 → ISR 연속성 유지
    interrupts();
    // enc_offset 설정이 임계구역 안에서 완료됨
}
```

이 방법은 `enc_count`를 리셋하지 않으므로 ISR과의 충돌 자체가 없습니다.

---

## 문제 4. float vs double 정밀도

### 4-1. 현재 코드에서의 영향

```
float  단정밀도: 유효숫자 ~7자리, 분해능 0.088°에서 충분
double 배정밀도: 유효숫자 ~15자리
```

4096 CPR 기준 최소 각도 단위 = 0.0878°. float의 유효숫자 7자리는 이를 충분히 표현합니다. **float 자체는 이 응용에서 정밀도 문제가 없습니다.**

다만 JSON 직렬화 시 `comPrint(deg, 2)`가 소수점 2자리로 제한하므로 실제 전달 정밀도는 0.01°입니다. 이는 인코더 분해능(0.088°)보다 세밀하므로 **출력 포맷 자체는 문제없습니다.**

---

## 문제 5. 추가 발견 문제

### 5-1. ISR 진입 조건 — 단일 엣지 vs 양 엣지

```c
// 현재 코드가 어떻게 인터럽트를 연결했는지에 따라 다름
attachInterrupt(ENC_PIN_A, enc_ab_isr, CHANGE);
attachInterrupt(ENC_PIN_B, enc_ab_isr, CHANGE);
```

만약 A, B 모두 CHANGE 인터럽트로 등록했다면:
A 변화 → ISR 진입 → A, B 읽기 → 테이블 참조 — 정상
B 변화 → ISR 진입 → A, B 읽기 — 정상

**그러나 단일 핀만 등록했다면** (예: A만 CHANGE), 카운트가 2배 감소하여 실제 분해능이 절반이 됩니다. 코드에 인터럽트 등록 부분이 없어 확인 불가 → 두 핀 모두 CHANGE로 등록되었는지 확인 필요.

### 5-2. `enc_last_state` 초기화 누락

```c
uint8_t enc_last_state;  // 전역 변수, 초기값 0

void setup() {
  // enc_last_state를 실제 핀 상태로 초기화하지 않으면
  // 첫 번째 ISR 호출 시 잘못된 상태 전이 발생
}
```

**수정:**

```c
void setup() {
  // ...
  uint8_t a = digitalRead(ENC_PIN_A);
  uint8_t b = digitalRead(ENC_PIN_B);
  enc_last_state = (b << 1) | a;  // 실제 초기 상태로 설정
}
```

### 5-3. `enc_count` 읽기의 원자성 (ESP32 특유 문제)

ESP32는 32비트 아키텍처이므로 `int32_t` 읽기는 단일 명령으로 원자적입니다. `noInterrupts()`/`interrupts()` 보호는 엄밀히 필요하지 않지만, 컴파일러 최적화 방지를 위해 `volatile`과 함께 유지하는 것이 올바릅니다. 현재 코드는 이 부분 정상.

---

## 수정 우선순위 요약

| 순위 | 문제 | 심각도 | 영향 |
|------|------|--------|------|
| 1 | `digitalRead` 2회 비원자적 읽기 | 높음 | 고속 회전 시 count 오류, 실제 각도 오차 |
| 2 | C# 360° 경계 wrap 미처리 | 높음 | ZERO 근처 통전 시 각도가 ~360° 오차 |
| 3 | ZERO race condition | 중간 | 초기화 직후 count=0 보장 안 됨 |
| 4 | `enc_last_state` 초기화 누락 | 낮음 | 최초 1~2 엣지 오검출 |
| 5 | 인터럽트 등록 핀 수 확인 | 확인 필요 | A만 등록 시 분해능 절반 |

**권장 수정 우선순위**: ISR을 GPIO 레지스터 원자 읽기로 변경하거나 PCNT 라이브러리로 교체 → C# wrap 처리 추가 → ZERO 명령 수정.
